26: 27: Wagtail is an open source content management system built on Django, with a strong community and commercial support. It's focused on user experience, and offers precise control for designers and developers. 28: 29: ![Wagtail screenshot](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/wagtail/wagtail@main/.github/wagtail-screenshot-with-browser.png) 30: 31: ### 🔥 Features 32: 33: - A fast, attractive interface for authors 34: - Complete control over front-end design and structure 35: - Scales to millions of pages and thousands of editors 36: - Fast out of the box, cache-friendly when you need it 37: - Content API for 'headless' sites with decoupled front-end 38: - Runs on a Raspberry Pi or a multi-datacenter cloud platform 39: - StreamField encourages flexible content without compromising structure 40: - Powerful, integrated search, using Elasticsearch or PostgreSQL 41: - Excellent support for images and embedded content 42: - Multi-site and multi-language ready 43: - Embraces and extends Django 44: 45: Find out more at [wagtail.org](https://wagtail.org/). 46: 47: ### 👉 Getting started 48: 49: Wagtail works with [Python 3](https://www.python.org/downloads/), on any platform. 50: 51: To get started with using Wagtail, run the following in a [virtual environment](https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/venv.html): 52: 53: ![Installing Wagtail](.github/install-animation.gif) 54: 55: ```sh 56: pip install wagtail 57: wagtail start mysite 58: cd mysite 59: pip install -r requirements.txt 60: python manage.py migrate 61: python manage.py createsuperuser 62: python manage.py runserver 63: ``` 64: 65: For detailed installation and setup docs, see [the getting started tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/tutorial.html). 66: 67: ### 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Who’s using it? 68: 69: Wagtail is used by [NASA](https://www.nasa.gov/), [Google](https://www.google.com/), [Oxfam](https://www.oxfam.org/en), the [NHS](https://www.nhs.uk/), [Mozilla](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/), [MIT](https://www.mit.edu/), the [Red Cross](https://www.icrc.org/en), [Salesforce](https://www.salesforce.com/), [NBC](https://www.nbc.com/), [BMW](https://www.bmw.com/en/index.html), and the US and UK governments. Add your own Wagtail site to [madewithwagtail.org](https://madewithwagtail.org). 70: 71: ### 📖 Documentation 72: 73: [docs.wagtail.org](https://docs.wagtail.org/) is the full reference for Wagtail, and includes guides for developers, designers and editors, alongside [release notes](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/releases/) and our [roadmap](https://wagtail.org/roadmap/). 74: 75: For those who are **new to Wagtail**, the [Zen of Wagtail](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/the_zen_of_wagtail.html) will help you understand what Wagtail is, and what Wagtail is _not_. 76: 77: **For developers** who are ready to jump in to their first Wagtail website the [Getting Started Tutorial](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/tutorial.html) will guide you through creating and editing your first page. 78: 79: **Do you have an existing Django project?** The [Wagtail Integration documentation](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/getting_started/integrating_into_django.html) is the best place to start. 80: 81: ### 📌 Compatibility 82: 83: _(If you are reading this on GitHub, the details here may not be indicative of the current released version - please see [Compatible Django / Python versions](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/releases/upgrading.html#compatible-django-python-versions) in the Wagtail documentation.)_ 84: 85: Wagtail supports: 86: 87: - Django 5.2.x and 6.0.x 88: - Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, and 3.14 89: - PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB and SQLite (with JSON1) as database backends 90: 91: [Previous versions of Wagtail](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/stable/releases/upgrading.html#compatible-django-python-versions) additionally supported Python 2.7, 3.8 and earlier Django versions. 92: 93: --- 94: 95: ### 📢 Community Support 96: 97: There is an active community of Wagtail users and developers responding to questions on [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/wagtail). When posting questions, please read Stack Overflow's advice on [how to ask questions](https://stackoverflow.com/help/how-to-ask) and remember to tag your question "wagtail". 98: 99: For topics and discussions that do not fit Stack Overflow's question and answer format we have a [Slack workspace](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/wiki/Slack). Please respect the time and effort of volunteers by not asking the same question in multiple places. 100: 101: [![Join slack community](.github/join-slack-community.png)](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/wiki/Slack) 102: 103: Our [GitHub discussion boards](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/discussions) are open for sharing ideas and plans for the Wagtail project. 104: 105: We maintain a curated list of third party packages, articles and other resources at [Awesome Wagtail](https://github.com/springload/awesome-wagtail). 106: 107: ### 🧑‍💼 Commercial Support 108: 109: Wagtail is sponsored by [Torchbox](https://torchbox.com/). If you need help implementing or hosting Wagtail, please contact us: hello@torchbox.com. See also [madewithwagtail.org/developers/](https://madewithwagtail.org/developers/) for expert Wagtail developers around the world. 110: 111: ### 🔐 Security 112: 113: We take the security of Wagtail, and related packages we maintain, seriously. If you have found a security issue with any of our projects please email us at [security@wagtail.org](mailto:security@wagtail.org) so we can work together to find and patch the issue. We appreciate responsible disclosure with any security related issues, so please contact us first before creating a GitHub issue. 114: 115: ### 🕒 Release schedule 116: 117: Feature releases of Wagtail are released every three months. Selected releases are designated as Long Term Support (LTS) releases, and will receive maintenance updates for an extended period to address any security and data-loss related issues. For dates of past and upcoming releases and support periods, see [Release Schedule](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/wiki/Release-schedule). 118: 119: #### 🕛 Nightly releases 120: 121: To try out the latest features before a release, we also create builds from `main` every night. You can find instructions on how to install the latest nightly release at https://releases.wagtail.org/nightly/index.html 122: 123: ### 🙋🏽 Contributing 124: 125: If you're a Python or Django developer, fork the repo and get stuck in! We have several developer focused channels on the [Slack workspace](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/wiki/Slack). 126: 127: You might like to start by reviewing the [contributing guidelines](https://docs.wagtail.org/en/latest/contributing/index.html) and checking issues with the [good first issue](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/labels/good%20first%20issue) label. 128: 129: We also welcome translations for Wagtail's interface. Translation work should be submitted through [Transifex](https://explore.transifex.com/torchbox/wagtail/). 130: 131: ### 🔓 License 132: 133: [BSD](https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/blob/main/LICENSE) - Free to use and modify for any purpose, including both open and closed-source code. 134: 135: ### 👏 Thanks 136: 137: We thank the following organisations for their services used in Wagtail's development: 138: 139: [![Browserstack](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/wagtail/wagtail@main/.github/browserstack-logo.svg)](https://www.browserstack.com/)
140: [BrowserStack](https://www.browserstack.com/) provides the project with free access to their live web-based browser testing tool, and automated Selenium cloud testing. 141: 142: [![Assistiv Labs](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/wagtail/wagtail@main/.github/assistivlabs-logo.png)](https://assistivlabs.com/)
143: [Assistiv Labs](https://assistivlabs.com/) provides the project with unlimited access to their remote testing with assistive technologies. (End of file - total 143 lines)

92: @receiver(pre_validate_delete, sender=Locale)
93: def reassign_root_page_locale_on_delete(sender, instance, **kwargs):
94:     # if we're deleting the locale used on the root page node, reassign that to a new locale first
95:     root_page_with_this_locale = Page.objects.filter(depth=1, locale=instance)
96:     if root_page_with_this_locale.exists():
97:         # Select the default locale, if one exists and isn't the one being deleted
98:         try:
99:             new_locale = Locale.get_default()
100:             default_locale_is_ok = new_locale != instance
101:         except (Locale.DoesNotExist, LookupError):
102:             default_locale_is_ok = False
103: 
104:         if not default_locale_is_ok:
105:             # fall back on any remaining locale
106:             new_locale = Locale.all_objects.exclude(pk=instance.pk).first()
107: 
108:         root_page_with_this_locale.update(locale=new_locale)

144: class BasePageManager(MP_NodeManager):
145:     def get_queryset(self):
146:         return self._queryset_class(self.model).order_by("path")
147: 
148:     def get_by_natural_key(self, url_path):
149:         """Get page by URL"""
150:         return self.get(url_path=url_path)
151: 
152:     def first_common_ancestor_of(self, pages, include_self=False, strict=False):
153:         """
154:         This is similar to ``PageQuerySet.first_common_ancestor`` but works
155:         for a list of pages instead of a queryset.
156:         """
157:         if not pages:
158:             if strict:
159:                 raise self.model.DoesNotExist("Can not find ancestor of empty list")
160:             return self.model.get_first_root_node()
161: 
162:         if include_self:
163:             paths = list({page.path for page in pages})
164:         else:
165:             paths = list({page.path[: -self.model.steplen] for page in pages})
166: 
167:         # This method works on anything, not just file system paths.
168:         common_parent_path = posixpath.commonprefix(paths)
169:         extra_chars = len(common_parent_path) % self.model.steplen
170:         if extra_chars != 0:
171:             common_parent_path = common_parent_path[:-extra_chars]
172: 
173:         if common_parent_path == "":
174:             if strict:
175:                 raise self.model.DoesNotExist("No common ancestor found!")
176: 
177:             return self.model.get_first_root_node()
178: 
179:         return self.get(path=common_parent_path)
180: 
181:     def annotate_parent_page(self, pages):
182:         """
183:         Annotates each page with its parent page. This is implemented as a
184:         manager-only method instead of a QuerySet method so it can be used with
185:         search results.
186: 
187:         If given a QuerySet, this method will evaluate it. Only use this method
188:         when you are ready to consume the queryset, e.g. after pagination has
189:         been applied. This is typically done in the view's ``get_context_data``
190:         using ``context["object_list"]``.
191: 
192:         This method does not return a new queryset, but modifies the existing one,
193:         to ensure any references to the queryset in the view's context are updated
194:         (e.g. when using ``context_object_name``).
195:         """
196:         parent_page_paths = {
197:             Page._get_parent_path_from_path(page.path) for page in pages
198:         }
199:         parent_pages_by_path = {
200:             page.path: page
201:             for page in Page.objects.filter(path__in=parent_page_paths).specific(
202:                 defer=True
203:             )
204:         }
205:         for page in pages:
206:             parent_page = parent_pages_by_path.get(
207:                 Page._get_parent_path_from_path(page.path)
208:             )
209:             page._parent_page = parent_page

215: class PageBase(models.base.ModelBase):
216:     """Metaclass for Page"""

218:     def __init__(cls, name, bases, dct):
219:         super().__init__(name, bases, dct)
220: 
221:         if "template" not in dct:
222:             # Define a default template path derived from the app name and model name
223:             cls.template = "{}/{}.html".format(
224:                 cls._meta.app_label,
225:                 camelcase_to_underscore(name),
226:             )
227: 
228:         if "ajax_template" not in dct:
229:             cls.ajax_template = None
230: 
231:         cls._clean_subpage_models = (
232:             None  # to be filled in on first call to cls.clean_subpage_models
233:         )
234:         cls._clean_parent_page_models = (
235:             None  # to be filled in on first call to cls.clean_parent_page_models
236:         )
237: 
238:         # All pages should be creatable unless explicitly set otherwise.
239:         # This attribute is not inheritable.
240:         if "is_creatable" not in dct:
241:             cls.is_creatable = not cls._meta.abstract
242: 
243:         if not cls._meta.abstract:
244:             # register this type in the list of page content types
245:             PAGE_MODEL_CLASSES.append(cls)

248: class AbstractPage(
249:     WorkflowMixin,
250:     PreviewableMixin,
251:     DraftStateMixin,
252:     LockableMixin,
253:     RevisionMixin,
254:     TranslatableMixin,
255:     SpecificMixin,
256:     MP_Node,
257: ):
258:     """
259:     Abstract superclass for Page. According to Django's inheritance rules, managers set on
260:     abstract models are inherited by subclasses, but managers set on concrete models that are extended
261:     via multi-table inheritance are not. We therefore need to attach PageManager to an abstract
262:     superclass to ensure that it is retained by subclasses of Page.
263:     """
264: 
265:     objects = PageManager()
266: 
267:     class Meta:
268:         abstract = True

272: # so that we can follow the same order when querying the Permission objects.
273: PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES = [

275:     ("bulk_delete_page", _("Bulk delete"), _("Delete pages with children")),
276:     ("change_page", _("Edit"), _("Edit any page")),
277:     ("lock_page", _("Lock"), _("Lock/unlock pages you've locked")),
278:     ("publish_page", _("Publish"), _("Publish any page")),
279:     ("unlock_page", _("Unlock"), _("Unlock any page")),
280: ]
281: 
282: PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPE_CHOICES = [
283:     (identifier[:-5], long_label) for identifier, _, long_label in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES
284: ]
285: 
286: PAGE_PERMISSION_CODENAMES = [identifier for identifier, *_ in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES]

298:     slug = models.SlugField(

300:         allow_unicode=True,
301:         max_length=255,
302:         help_text=_(
303:             "The name of the page as it will appear in URLs e.g http://domain.com/blog/[my-slug]/"
304:         ),

307:         ContentType,
308:         verbose_name=_("content type"),
309:         related_name="pages",
310:         on_delete=models.SET(get_default_page_content_type),
311:     )

500:         """

525:     @staticmethod
526:     def find_for_request(request: HttpRequest, path: str) -> Page | None:
527:         """
528:         Find the page for the given HTTP request object, and URL path. The full
529:         page route will be cached via ``request._wagtail_route_for_request``.
530:         """
531:         result = Page.route_for_request(request, path)
532:         if result is not None:
533:             return result[0]

566:         # base_content_type so that we can query for page revisions without
567:         # having to know the specific Page type.

577:     def set_url_path(self, parent):
578:         """
579:         Populate the url_path field based on this page's slug and the specified parent page.
580:         (We pass a parent in here, rather than retrieving it via get_parent, so that we can give
581:         new unsaved pages a meaningful URL when previewing them; at that point the page has not
582:         been assigned a position in the tree, as far as treebeard is concerned.
583:         """
584:         if parent:
585:             self.url_path = parent.url_path + self.slug + "/"
586:         else:
587:             # a page without a parent is the tree root, which always has a url_path of '/'
588:             self.url_path = "/"
589: 
590:         return self.url_path
591: 
592:     @staticmethod
593:     def _slug_is_available(slug, parent_page, page=None):
594:         """
595:         Determine whether the given slug is available for use on a child page of
596:         parent_page. If 'page' is passed, the slug is intended for use on that page
597:         (and so it will be excluded from the duplicate check).
598:         """
599:         if parent_page is None:
600:             # the root page's slug can be whatever it likes...
601:             return True
602: 
603:         siblings = parent_page.get_children()
604:         if page:
605:             siblings = siblings.not_page(page)
606: 
607:         return not siblings.filter(slug=slug).exists()
608: 
609:     def _get_autogenerated_slug(self, base_slug):
610:         candidate_slug = base_slug
611:         suffix = 1
612:         parent_page = self.get_parent()
613: 
614:         while not Page._slug_is_available(candidate_slug, parent_page, self):
615:             # try with incrementing suffix until we find a slug which is available
616:             suffix += 1
617:             candidate_slug = "%s-%d" % (base_slug, suffix)
618: 
619:         return candidate_slug
620: 
621:     def get_default_locale(self):
622:         """
623:         Finds the default locale to use for this page.
624: 
625:         This will be called just before the initial save.
626:         """
627:         parent = self.get_parent()
628:         if parent is not None:
629:             return (
630:                 parent.specific_class.objects.defer()
631:                 .select_related("locale")
632:                 .get(id=parent.id)
633:                 .locale
634:             )
635: 
636:         return super().get_default_locale()
637: 
638:     def get_admin_default_ordering(self):
639:         """
640:         Determine the default ordering for child pages in the admin index listing.
641:         Returns a string (e.g. 'latest_revision_created_at, title, ord' or 'live').
642:         """
643:         return self.admin_default_ordering

645:     def _set_core_field_defaults(self):
646:         """
647:         Set default values for core fields (slug, draft_title, locale) that need to be
648:         in place before validating or saving
649:         """
650: 
651:         # If path is unset, then this cleaning step must be happening in advance of the
652:         # final save, and there is no point trying to auto-generate a slug yet, as we
653:         # don't know the set of sibling pages to de-duplicate against. In this case,
654:         # this method will be called again as part of the call to `save()` after the
655:         # path is populated, and we will auto-generate the slug at that point if
656:         # necessary.
657:         if (not self.slug) and self.path:
658:             # Try to auto-populate slug from title
659:             allow_unicode = getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_ALLOW_UNICODE_SLUGS", True)
660:             base_slug = slugify(self.title, allow_unicode=allow_unicode)
661: 
662:             # only proceed if we get a non-empty base slug back from slugify
663:             if base_slug:
664:                 self.slug = self._get_autogenerated_slug(base_slug)
665: 
666:         if not self.draft_title:
667:             self.draft_title = self.title
668: 
669:         # Set the locale
670:         if self.locale_id is None:
671:             self.locale = self.get_default_locale()
672: 
673:     def full_clean(self, *args, **kwargs):
674:         self._set_core_field_defaults()
675:         super().full_clean(*args, **kwargs)
676: 
677:     def _check_slug_is_unique(self):
678:         parent_page = self.get_parent()
679:         if not Page._slug_is_available(self.slug, parent_page, self):
680:             raise ValidationError(
681:                 {
682:                     "slug": _(
683:                         "The slug '%(page_slug)s' is already in use within the parent page at '%(parent_url_path)s'."
684:                     )
685:                     % {"page_slug": self.slug, "parent_url_path": parent_page.url}
686:                 }
687:             )
688: 
689:     def clean(self):
690:         super().clean()
691:         self._check_slug_is_unique()
692: 
693:     def minimal_clean(self):
694:         self._set_core_field_defaults()
695:         self.title = self._meta.get_field("title").clean(self.title, self)
696:         self._check_slug_is_unique()

698:     def is_site_root(self):
699:         """
700:         Returns True if this page is the root of any site.
701: 
702:         This includes translations of site root pages as well.
703:         """
704:         # `_is_site_root` may be populated by `annotate_site_root_state` on `PageQuerySet` as a
705:         # performance optimisation
706:         if hasattr(self, "_is_site_root"):
707:             return self._is_site_root
708: 
709:         return Site.objects.filter(
710:             root_page__translation_key=self.translation_key
711:         ).exists()

713:     @transaction.atomic
714:     # ensure that changes are only committed when we have updated all descendant URL paths, to preserve consistency
715:     def save(self, clean=True, user=None, log_action=False, **kwargs):
716:         """
717:         Writes the page to the database, performing additional housekeeping tasks to ensure data
718:         integrity:
719: 
720:         * ``locale``, ``draft_title`` and ``slug`` are set to default values if not provided, with ``slug``
721:           being generated from the title with a suffix to ensure uniqueness within the parent page
722:           where necessary
723:         * The ``url_path`` field is set based on the ``slug`` and the parent page
724:         * If the ``slug`` has changed, the ``url_path`` of this page and all descendants is updated and
725:           a :ref:`page_slug_changed` signal is sent
726: 
727:         New pages should be saved by passing the unsaved page instance to the
728:         :meth:`~treebeard.mp_tree.MP_Node.add_child`
729:         or :meth:`~treebeard.mp_tree.MP_Node.add_sibling` method of an existing page, which will correctly update
730:         the fields responsible for tracking the page's location in the tree.
731: 
732:         If ``clean=False`` is passed, the page is saved without validation. This is appropriate for updates that only
733:         change metadata such as `latest_revision` while keeping content and page location unchanged.
734: 
735:         If ``clean=True`` is passed (the default), and the page has ``live=True`` set, the page is validated using
736:         :meth:`~django.db.models.Model.full_clean` before saving.
737: 
738:         If ``clean=True`` is passed, and the page has ``live=False`` set, only the title and slug fields are validated.
739:         """
740:         if clean:
741:             if self.live:
742:                 self.full_clean()
743:             else:
744:                 # Saving as draft; only perform the minimal validation to satisfy data integrity
745:                 self.minimal_clean()
746: 
747:         slug_changed = False
748:         is_new = self.id is None
749: 
750:         if is_new:
751:             # we are creating a record. If we're doing things properly, this should happen
752:             # through a treebeard method like add_child, in which case the 'path' field
753:             # has been set and so we can safely call get_parent
754:             self.set_url_path(self.get_parent())
755:         else:
756:             # Check that we are committing the slug to the database
757:             # Basically: If update_fields has been specified, and slug is not included, skip this step
758:             if not (
759:                 "update_fields" in kwargs
760:                 and kwargs["update_fields"] is not None
761:                 and "slug" not in kwargs["update_fields"]
762:             ):
763:                 # see if the slug has changed from the record in the db, in which case we need to
764:                 # update url_path of self and all descendants. Even though we might not need it,
765:                 # the specific page is fetched here for sending to the 'page_slug_changed' signal.
766:                 old_record = Page.objects.get(id=self.id).specific
767:                 if old_record.slug != self.slug:
768:                     self.set_url_path(self.get_parent())
769:                     slug_changed = True
770:                     old_url_path = old_record.url_path
771:                     new_url_path = self.url_path
772: 
773:         result = super().save(**kwargs)
774: 
775:         if slug_changed:
776:             self._update_descendant_url_paths(old_url_path, new_url_path)
777:             # Emit page_slug_changed signal on successful db commit
778:             transaction.on_commit(
779:                 lambda: page_slug_changed.send(
780:                     sender=self.specific_class or self.__class__,
781:                     instance=self.specific,
782:                     instance_before=old_record,
783:                 )
784:             )
785: 
786:         # Check if this is a root page of any sites and clear the 'wagtail_site_root_paths' key if so
787:         # Note: New translations of existing site roots are considered site roots as well, so we must
788:         # always check if this page is a site root, even if it's new.
789:         if self.is_site_root():
790:             Site.clear_site_root_paths_cache()
791: 
792:         # Log
793:         if is_new:
794:             cls = type(self)
795:             logger.info(
796:                 'Page created: "%s" id=%d content_type=%s.%s path=%s',
797:                 self.title,
798:                 self.id,
799:                 cls._meta.app_label,
800:                 cls.__name__,
801:                 self.url_path,
802:             )
803: 
804:         if log_action is not None:
805:             # The default for log_action is False. i.e. don't log unless specifically instructed
806:             # Page creation is a special case that we want logged by default, but allow skipping it
807:             # explicitly by passing log_action=None
808:             if is_new:
809:                 log(
810:                     instance=self,
811:                     action="wagtail.create",
812:                     user=user or self.owner,
813:                     content_changed=True,
814:                 )
815:             elif log_action:
816:                 log(instance=self, action=log_action, user=user)
817: 
818:         return result

824:     @classmethod
825:     def check(cls, **kwargs):
826:         errors = super().check(**kwargs)
827: 
828:         # Check that foreign keys from pages are not configured to cascade
829:         # This is the default Django behaviour which must be explicitly overridden
830:         # to prevent pages disappearing unexpectedly and the tree being corrupted
831: 
832:         # get names of foreign keys pointing to parent classes (such as page_ptr)
833:         field_exceptions = [
834:             field.name
835:             for model in [cls] + list(cls._meta.get_parent_list())
836:             for field in model._meta.parents.values()
837:             if field
838:         ]
839: 
840:         for field in cls._meta.fields:
841:             if (
842:                 isinstance(field, models.ForeignKey)
843:                 and field.name not in field_exceptions
844:             ):
845:                 if field.remote_field.on_delete == models.CASCADE:
846:                     errors.append(
847:                         checks.Warning(
848:                             "Field hasn't specified on_delete action",
849:                             hint="Set on_delete=models.SET_NULL and make sure the field is nullable or set on_delete=models.PROTECT. Wagtail does not allow simple database CASCADE because it will corrupt its tree storage.",
850:                             obj=field,
851:                             id="wagtailcore.W001",
852:                         )
853:                     )
854: 
855:         if not isinstance(cls.objects, PageManager):
856:             errors.append(
857:                 checks.Error(
858:                     "Manager does not inherit from PageManager",
859:                     hint="Ensure that custom Page managers inherit from wagtail.models.PageManager",
860:                     obj=cls,
861:                     id="wagtailcore.E002",
862:                 )
863:             )
864: 
865:         try:
866:             cls.clean_subpage_models()
867:         except (ValueError, LookupError) as e:
868:             errors.append(
869:                 checks.Error(
870:                     "Invalid subpage_types setting for %s" % cls,
871:                     hint=str(e),
872:                     id="wagtailcore.E002",
873:                 )
874:             )
875: 
876:         try:
877:             cls.clean_parent_page_models()
878:         except (ValueError, LookupError) as e:
879:             errors.append(
880:                 checks.Error(
881:                     "Invalid parent_page_types setting for %s" % cls,
882:                     hint=str(e),
883:                     id="wagtailcore.E002",
884:                 )
885:             )
886: 
887:         return errors
888: 
889:     def _update_descendant_url_paths(self, old_url_path, new_url_path):
890:         (
891:             Page.objects.filter(path__startswith=self.path)
892:             .exclude(pk=self.pk)
893:             .update(
894:                 url_path=Concat(
895:                     Value(new_url_path), Substr("url_path", len(old_url_path) + 1)
896:                 )
897:             )
898:         )
899: 
900:     @property
901:     def page_type_display_name(self):
902:         """
903:         A human-readable version of this page's type.
904:         """
905:         if not self.specific_class or self.is_root():
906:             return ""
907:         else:
908:             return self.specific_class.get_verbose_name()
909: 
910:     def route(self, request, path_components):
911:         if path_components:
912:             # request is for a child of this page
913:             child_slug = path_components[0]
914:             remaining_components = path_components[1:]
915: 
916:             try:
917:                 subpage = self.get_children().get(slug=child_slug)
918:                 # Cache the parent page on the subpage to avoid another db query
919:                 # Treebeard's get_parent will use the `_cached_parent_obj` attribute if it exists
920:                 # And update = False
921:                 subpage._cached_parent_obj = self
922: 
923:             except Page.DoesNotExist as e:
924:                 raise Http404 from e
925: 
926:             return subpage.specific.route(request, remaining_components)
927: 
928:         else:
929:             # request is for this very page
930:             if self.live:
931:                 return RouteResult(self)
932:             else:
933:                 raise Http404
934: 
935:     def get_admin_display_title(self):
936:         """
937:         Return the title for this page as it should appear in the admin backend;
938:         override this if you wish to display extra contextual information about the page,
939:         such as language. By default, returns ``draft_title``.
940:         """
941:         # Fall back on title if draft_title is blank (which may happen if the page was created
942:         # in a fixture or migration that didn't explicitly handle draft_title)
943:         return self.draft_title or self.title
944: 
945:     @transaction.atomic
946:     def save_revision(
947:         self,
948:         user=None,
949:         approved_go_live_at=None,
950:         changed=True,
951:         log_action=False,
952:         previous_revision=None,
953:         clean=True,
954:         overwrite_revision=None,
955:     ):
956:         # Raise error if this is not the specific version of the page
957:         if not isinstance(self, self.specific_class):
958:             raise RuntimeError(
959:                 "page.save_revision() must be called on the specific version of the page. "
960:                 "Call page.specific.save_revision() instead."
961:             )
962: 
963:         # Raise an error if this page is an alias.
964:         if self.alias_of_id:
965:             raise RuntimeError(
966:                 "page.save_revision() was called on an alias page. "
967:                 "Revisions are not required for alias pages as they are an exact copy of another page."
968:             )
969: 
970:         if clean:
971:             self.full_clean()
972: 
973:         new_comments = getattr(self, COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME).filter(pk__isnull=True)
974:         for comment in new_comments:
975:             # We need to ensure comments have an id in the revision, so positions can be identified correctly
976:             comment.save()
977: 
978:         if overwrite_revision:
979:             # the revision being overwritten must be the latest revision for the current page, and must match
980:             # the current user (if any)
981:             latest_revision = self.get_latest_revision()
982:             if overwrite_revision != latest_revision:
983:                 raise PermissionDenied(
984:                     gettext(
985:                         "Cannot overwrite a revision that is not the latest for "
986:                         "this %(model_name)s."
987:                     )
988:                     % {"model_name": Page._meta.verbose_name}
989:                 )
990: 
991:             if overwrite_revision.user_id != (user and user.pk):
992:                 raise PermissionDenied(
993:                     gettext(
994:                         "Cannot overwrite a revision that was not created "
995:                         "by the current user."
996:                     )
997:                 )

999:             overwrite_revision.created_at = timezone.now()

1: from collections import namedtuple
2: 
3: from django.apps import apps
4: from django.conf import settings
5: from django.contrib.auth.models import Group, Permission
6: from django.core.cache import cache
7: from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError
8: from django.db import models
9: from django.db.models import Case, IntegerField, Q, When
10: from django.db.models.functions import Lower
11: from django.http.request import split_domain_port
12: from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _
13: 
14: MATCH_HOSTNAME_PORT = 0
15: MATCH_HOSTNAME_DEFAULT = 1
16: MATCH_DEFAULT = 2
17: MATCH_HOSTNAME = 3
18: 
19: 
20: def get_site_for_hostname(hostname, port):
21:     """Return the wagtailcore.Site object for the given hostname and port."""
22:     Site = apps.get_model("wagtailcore.Site")
23: 
24:     sites = list(
25:         Site.objects.annotate(
26:             match=Case(
27:                 # annotate the results by best choice descending
28:                 # put exact hostname+port match first
29:                 When(hostname=hostname, port=port, then=MATCH_HOSTNAME_PORT),
30:                 # then put hostname+default (better than just hostname or just default)
31:                 When(
32:                     hostname=hostname, is_default_site=True, then=MATCH_HOSTNAME_DEFAULT
33:                 ),
34:                 # then match default with different hostname. there is only ever
35:                 # one default, so order it above (possibly multiple) hostname
36:                 # matches so we can use sites[0] below to access it
37:                 When(is_default_site=True, then=MATCH_DEFAULT),
38:                 # because of the filter below, if it's not default then its a hostname match
39:                 default=MATCH_HOSTNAME,
40:                 output_field=IntegerField(),
41:             )
42:         )
43:         .filter(Q(hostname=hostname) | Q(is_default_site=True))
44:         .order_by("match")
45:         .select_related("root_page")
46:     )
47: 
48:     if sites:
49:         # if there's a unique match or hostname (with port or default) match
50:         if len(sites) == 1 or sites[0].match in (
51:             MATCH_HOSTNAME_PORT,
52:             MATCH_HOSTNAME_DEFAULT,
53:         ):
54:             return sites[0]
55: 
56:         # if there is a default match with a different hostname, see if
57:         # there are many hostname matches. if only 1 then use that instead
58:         # otherwise we use the default
59:         if sites[0].match == MATCH_DEFAULT:
60:             return sites[len(sites) == 2]
61: 
62:     raise Site.DoesNotExist()
63: 
64:

67:         return (
68:             super()
69:             .get_queryset()
70:             .order_by(
71:                 Case(
72:                     When(site_name="", then=Lower("hostname")),
73:                     default=Lower("site_name"),
74:                 ),
75:                 Lower("hostname"),
76:             )
77:         )
78: 
79:     def get_by_natural_key(self, hostname, port):
80:         return self.get(hostname=hostname, port=port)

86: # Increase the cache version whenever the structure SiteRootPath tuple changes

90: class Site(models.Model):
91:     hostname = models.CharField(
92:         verbose_name=_("hostname"), max_length=255, db_index=True
93:     )
94:     port = models.IntegerField(
95:         verbose_name=_("port"),
96:         default=80,
97:         help_text=_(
98:             "Set this to something other than 80 if you need a specific port number to appear in URLs"
99:             " (e.g. development on port 8000). Does not affect request handling (so port forwarding still works)."
100:         ),
101:     )
102:     site_name = models.CharField(
103:         verbose_name=_("site name"),
104:         max_length=255,
105:         blank=True,
106:         help_text=_("Human-readable name for the site."),
107:     )
108:     root_page = models.ForeignKey(
109:         "Page",
110:         verbose_name=_("root page"),
111:         related_name="sites_rooted_here",
112:         on_delete=models.CASCADE,
113:     )
114:     is_default_site = models.BooleanField(
115:         verbose_name=_("is default site"),
116:         default=False,
117:         help_text=_(
118:             "If true, this site will handle requests for all other hostnames that do not have a site entry of their own"
119:         ),
120:     )
121: 
122:     objects = SiteManager()
123: 
124:     class Meta:
125:         unique_together = ("hostname", "port")
126:         verbose_name = _("site")
127:         verbose_name_plural = _("sites")
128: 
129:     def natural_key(self):
130:         return (self.hostname, self.port)
131: 
132:     def __str__(self):
133:         default_suffix = " [{}]".format(_("default"))
134:         if self.site_name:
135:             return self.site_name + (default_suffix if self.is_default_site else "")
136:         else:
137:             return (
138:                 self.hostname
139:                 + ("" if self.port == 80 else (":%d" % self.port))
140:                 + (default_suffix if self.is_default_site else "")
141:             )
142: 
143:     def clean(self):
144:         self.hostname = self.hostname.lower()
145: 
146:     @staticmethod
147:     def find_for_request(request):
148:         """
149:         Find the site object responsible for responding to this HTTP
150:         request object. Try:
151: 
152:         * unique hostname first
153:         * then hostname and port
154:         * if there is no matching hostname at all, or no matching
155:           hostname:port combination, fall back to the unique default site,
156:           or raise an exception
157: 
158:         NB this means that high-numbered ports on an extant hostname may
159:         still be routed to a different hostname which is set as the default
160: 
161:         The site will be cached via request._wagtail_site
162:         """
163: 
164:         if request is None:
165:             return None
166: 
167:         if not hasattr(request, "_wagtail_site"):
168:             site = Site._find_for_request(request)
169:             request._wagtail_site = site
170:         return request._wagtail_site
171: 
172:     @staticmethod
173:     def _find_for_request(request):
174:         # Use `_get_raw_host` to avoid ALLOWED_HOSTS checks
175:         hostname = split_domain_port(request._get_raw_host())[0]
176:         port = request.get_port()
177:         site = None
178:         try:
179:             site = get_site_for_hostname(hostname, port)
180:         except Site.DoesNotExist:
181:             pass
182:             # copy old SiteMiddleware behaviour
183:         return site
184: 
185:     @property
186:     def root_url(self):
187:         if self.port == 80:
188:             return "http://%s" % self.hostname
189:         elif self.port == 443:
190:             return "https://%s" % self.hostname
191:         else:
192:             return "http://%s:%d" % (self.hostname, self.port)
193: 
194:     def clean_fields(self, exclude=None):
195:         super().clean_fields(exclude)
196:         # Only one site can have the is_default_site flag set
197:         try:
198:             default = Site.objects.get(is_default_site=True)
199:         except Site.DoesNotExist:
200:             pass
201:         except Site.MultipleObjectsReturned:
202:             raise
203:         else:
204:             if self.is_default_site and self.pk != default.pk:
205:                 raise ValidationError(
206:                     {
207:                         "is_default_site": [
208:                             _(
209:                                 "%(hostname)s is already configured as the default site."
210:                                 " You must unset that before you can save this site as default."
211:                             )
212:                             % {"hostname": default.hostname}
213:                         ]
214:                     }
215:                 )
216: 
217:     @staticmethod
218:     def get_site_root_paths():
219:         """
220:         Return a list of `SiteRootPath` instances, most specific path
221:         first - used to translate url_paths into actual URLs with hostnames.
222: 
223:         Each root path is an instance of the `SiteRootPath` named tuple,
224:         and have the following attributes:
225: 
226:         - ``site_id`` - The ID of the Site record
227:         - ``root_path`` - The internal URL path of the site's home page (for example '/home/')
228:         - ``root_url`` - The scheme/domain name of the site (for example 'https://www.example.com/')
229:         - ``language_code`` - The language code of the site (for example 'en')
230:         """
231:         result = cache.get(
232:             SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_KEY, version=SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_VERSION
233:         )
234: 
235:         if result is None:
236:             result = []
237: 
238:             for site in Site.objects.select_related(
239:                 "root_page", "root_page__locale"
240:             ).order_by("-root_page__url_path", "-is_default_site", "hostname"):
241:                 if getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_I18N_ENABLED", False):
242:                     result.extend(
243:                         [
244:                             SiteRootPath(
245:                                 site.id,
246:                                 root_page.url_path,
247:                                 site.root_url,
248:                                 root_page.locale.language_code,
249:                             )
250:                             for root_page in site.root_page.get_translations(
251:                                 inclusive=True
252:                             ).select_related("locale")
253:                         ]
254:                     )
255:                 else:
256:                     result.append(
257:                         SiteRootPath(
258:                             site.id,
259:                             site.root_page.url_path,
260:                             site.root_url,
261:                             site.root_page.locale.language_code,
262:                         )
263:                     )
264: 
265:             cache.set(
266:                 SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_KEY,
267:                 result,
268:                 3600,
269:                 version=SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_VERSION,
270:             )
271: 
272:         else:
273:             # Convert the cache result to a list of SiteRootPath tuples, as some
274:             # cache backends (e.g. Redis) don't support named tuples.
275:             result = [SiteRootPath(*result) for result in result]
276: 
277:         return result
278: 
279:     @staticmethod
280:     def clear_site_root_paths_cache():
281:         cache.delete(SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_KEY, version=SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_VERSION)
282:

30: class RevisionQuerySet(models.QuerySet):
31:     def page_revisions_q(self):

34:     def page_revisions(self):

40:     def for_instance(self, instance):

42:             # Use RevisionMixin.get_base_content_type() if available

44:                 base_content_type=instance.get_base_content_type(),
45:                 object_id=str(instance.pk),

57: class RevisionsManager(models.Manager.from_queryset(RevisionQuerySet)):
58:     def previous_revision_id_subquery(self, revision_fk_name="revision"):
59:         """
60:         Returns a Subquery that can be used to annotate a queryset with the ID
61:         of the previous revision, based on the revision_fk_name field. Useful
62:         to avoid N+1 queries when generating comparison links between revisions.
63: 
64:         The logic is similar to ``Revision.get_previous().pk``.
65:         """
66:         fk = revision_fk_name
67:         return Subquery(
68:             Revision.objects.filter(
69:                 base_content_type_id=OuterRef(f"{fk}__base_content_type_id"),
70:                 object_id=OuterRef(f"{fk}__object_id"),
71:             )
72:             .filter(
73:                 Q(
74:                     created_at=OuterRef(f"{fk}__created_at"),
75:                     pk__lt=OuterRef(f"{fk}__pk"),
76:                 )
77:                 | Q(created_at__lt=OuterRef(f"{fk}__created_at"))
78:             )
79:             .order_by("-created_at", "-pk")
80:             .values_list("pk", flat=True)[:1]
81:         )

99:     )
100:     created_at = models.DateTimeField(db_index=True, verbose_name=_("created at"))
101:     user = models.ForeignKey(
102:         settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
103:         verbose_name=_("user"),
104:         null=True,
105:         blank=True,
106:         on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
107:         related_name="wagtail_revisions",

111:         verbose_name=_("content JSON"), encoder=DjangoJSONEncoder
112:     )
113:     approved_go_live_at = models.DateTimeField(
114:         verbose_name=_("approved go live at"), null=True, blank=True, db_index=True
115:     )

117:     objects = RevisionsManager()
118:     page_revisions = PageRevisionsManager()
119: 
120:     content_object = GenericForeignKey(
121:         "content_type", "object_id", for_concrete_model=False
122:     )
123: 
124:     wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
125: 
126:     @cached_property
127:     def base_content_object(self):
128:         return self.base_content_type.get_object_for_this_type(pk=self.object_id)
129: 
130:     def save(self, user=None, *args, **kwargs):
131:         # Set default value for created_at to now
132:         # We cannot use auto_now_add as that will override
133:         # any value that is set before saving
134:         if self.created_at is None:
135:             self.created_at = timezone.now()
136: 
137:         # Set default value for base_content_type to the content_type.
138:         # Page revisions should set this to the default Page model's content type,
139:         # but the distinction may not be necessary for models that do not use inheritance.
140:         if self.base_content_type_id is None:
141:             self.base_content_type_id = self.content_type_id
142: 
143:         super().save(*args, **kwargs)
144: 
145:         if (
146:             self.approved_go_live_at is None
147:             and "update_fields" in kwargs
148:             and "approved_go_live_at" in kwargs["update_fields"]
149:         ):
150:             # Log scheduled revision publish cancellation
151:             object = self.as_object()
152:             # go_live_at = kwargs['update_fields'][]
153:             log(
154:                 instance=object,
155:                 action="wagtail.schedule.cancel",
156:                 data={
157:                     "revision": {
158:                         "id": self.id,
159:                         "created": ensure_utc(self.created_at),
160:                         "go_live_at": ensure_utc(object.go_live_at)
161:                         if object.go_live_at
162:                         else None,
163:                         "has_live_version": object.live,
164:                     }
165:                 },
166:                 user=user,
167:                 revision=self,
168:             )
169: 
170:     def as_object(self):
171:         return self.content_object.with_content_json(self.content)
172: 
173:     def is_latest_revision(self):
174:         if self.id is None:
175:             # special case: a revision without an ID is presumed to be newly-created and is thus
176:             # newer than any revision that might exist in the database
177:             return True
178: 
179:         latest_revision_id = (
180:             Revision.objects.filter(
181:                 base_content_type_id=self.base_content_type_id,
182:                 object_id=self.object_id,
183:             )
184:             .order_by("-created_at", "-id")
185:             .values_list("id", flat=True)
186:             .first()
187:         )
188:         return latest_revision_id == self.id
189: 
190:     def delete(self):
191:         # Update revision_created fields for comments that reference the current revision, if applicable.
192: 
193:         try:
194:             next_revision = self.get_next()
195:         except Revision.DoesNotExist:
196:             next_revision = None
197: 
198:         if next_revision:
199:             # move comments created on this revision to the next revision, as they may well still apply if they're unresolved
200:             self.created_comments.all().update(revision_created=next_revision)
201: 
202:         return super().delete()
203: 
204:     def publish(
205:         self,
206:         user=None,
207:         changed=True,
208:         log_action=True,
209:         previous_revision=None,
210:         skip_permission_checks=False,
211:     ):
212:         return self.content_object.publish(
213:             self,
214:             user=user,
215:             changed=changed,
216:             log_action=log_action,
217:             previous_revision=previous_revision,
218:             skip_permission_checks=skip_permission_checks,
219:         )
220: 
221:     def get_previous(self):
222:         return self.get_previous_by_created_at(
223:             base_content_type_id=self.base_content_type_id,
224:             object_id=self.object_id,
225:         )

1098:     def update_aliases(self, *, revision=None, _content=None, _updated_ids=None):
1099:         """
1100:         Publishes all aliases that follow this page with the latest content from this page.
1101: 
1102:         This is called by Wagtail whenever a page with aliases is published.
1103: 
1104:         :param revision: The revision of the original page that we are updating to (used for logging purposes)
1105:         :type revision: Revision, Optional
1106:         """
1107:         specific_self = self.specific
1108: 
1109:         # Only compute this if necessary since it's quite a heavy operation
1110:         if _content is None:
1111:             _content = self.serializable_data()
1112: 
1113:         # A list of IDs that have already been updated. This is just in case someone has
1114:         # created an alias loop (which is impossible to do with the UI Wagtail provides)
1115:         _updated_ids = _updated_ids or []
1116: 
1117:         for alias in self.specific_class.objects.filter(alias_of=self).exclude(
1118:             id__in=_updated_ids
1119:         ):
1120:             # FIXME: Switch to the same fields that are excluded from copy
1121:             # We can't do this right now because we can't exclude fields from with_content_json
1122:             exclude_fields = [
1123:                 "id",
1124:                 "path",
1125:                 "depth",
1126:                 "numchild",
1127:                 "url_path",
1128:                 "path",
1129:                 "index_entries",
1130:                 "postgres_index_entries",
1131:             ]
1132: 
1133:             # Copy field content
1134:             alias_updated = alias.with_content_json(_content)
1135: 
1136:             # Publish the alias if it's currently in draft
1137:             alias_updated.live = True
1138:             alias_updated.has_unpublished_changes = False
1139: 
1140:             # Copy child relations
1141:             child_object_map = specific_self.copy_all_child_relations(
1142:                 target=alias_updated, exclude=exclude_fields
1143:             )
1144: 
1145:             # Process child objects
1146:             # This has two jobs:
1147:             #  - If the alias is in a different locale, this updates the
1148:             #    locale of any translatable child objects to match
1149:             #  - If the alias is not a translation of the original, this
1150:             #    changes the translation_key field of all child objects
1151:             #    so they do not clash
1152:             if child_object_map:
1153:                 alias_is_translation = alias.translation_key == self.translation_key
1154: 
1155:                 def process_child_object(child_object):
1156:                     if isinstance(child_object, TranslatableMixin):
1157:                         # Child object's locale must always match the page
1158:                         child_object.locale = alias_updated.locale
1159: 
1160:                         # If the alias isn't a translation of the original page,
1161:                         # change the child object's translation_keys so they are
1162:                         # not either
1163:                         if not alias_is_translation:
1164:                             child_object.translation_key = uuid.uuid4()
1165: 
1166:                 for (rel, previous_id), child_objects in child_object_map.items():
1167:                     if previous_id is None:
1168:                         for child_object in child_objects:
1169:                             process_child_object(child_object)
1170:                     else:
1171:                         process_child_object(child_objects)
1172: 
1173:             # Copy M2M relations
1174:             _copy_m2m_relations(
1175:                 specific_self, alias_updated, exclude_fields=exclude_fields
1176:             )
1177: 
1178:             # Don't change the aliases slug
1179:             # Aliases can have their own slugs so they can be siblings of the original
1180:             alias_updated.slug = alias.slug
1181:             alias_updated.set_url_path(alias_updated.get_parent())
1182: 
1183:             # Aliases don't have revisions, so update fields that would normally be updated by save_revision
1184:             alias_updated.draft_title = alias_updated.title
1185:             alias_updated.latest_revision_created_at = self.latest_revision_created_at
1186: 
1187:             alias_updated.save(clean=False)
1188: 
1189:             page_published.send(
1190:                 sender=alias_updated.specific_class,
1191:                 instance=alias_updated,
1192:                 revision=revision,
1193:                 alias=True,
1194:             )
1195: 
1196:             # Update any aliases of that alias
1197: 
1198:             # Design note:
1199:             # It could be argued that this will be faster if we just changed these alias-of-alias
1200:             # pages to all point to the original page and avoid having to update them recursively.
1201:             #
1202:             # But, it's useful to have a record of how aliases have been chained.
1203:             # For example, In Wagtail Localize, we use aliases to create mirrored trees, but those
1204:             # trees themselves could have aliases within them. If an alias within a tree is
1205:             # converted to a regular page, we want the alias in the mirrored tree to follow that
1206:             # new page and stop receiving updates from the original page.
1207:             #
1208:             # Doing it this way requires an extra lookup query per alias but this is small in
1209:             # comparison to the work required to update the alias.
1210: 
1211:             alias.update_aliases(
1212:                 revision=revision,
1213:                 _content=_content,
1214:                 _updated_ids=_updated_ids,
1215:             )
1216:

1280:     def check_request_method(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
1281:         """
1282:         Checks the ``method`` attribute of the request against those supported
1283:         by the page (as defined by :attr:`allowed_http_methods`) and responds
1284:         accordingly.
1285: 
1286:         If supported, ``None`` is returned, and the request is processed
1287:         normally. If not, a warning is logged and an ``HttpResponseNotAllowed``
1288:         is returned, and any further request handling is terminated.
1289:         """
1290:         allowed_methods = self.allowed_http_method_names()
1291:         if request.method not in allowed_methods:
1292:             response = HttpResponseNotAllowed(allowed_methods)
1293:             log_response(
1294:                 "Method Not Allowed (%s): %s",
1295:                 request.method,
1296:                 request.path,
1297:                 request=request,
1298:                 response=response,

1300:             return response

1302:     def handle_options_request(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
1303:         """
1304:         Returns an ``HttpResponse`` with an ``"Allow"`` header containing the list of
1305:         supported HTTP methods for this page. This method is used instead of
1306:         :meth:`serve` to handle requests when the ``OPTIONS`` HTTP verb is
1307:         detected (and :class:`HTTPMethod.OPTIONS <python:http.HTTPMethod>` is
1308:         present in :attr:`allowed_http_methods` for this type of page).

1310:         return HttpResponse(

1322:     def _get_site_root_paths(self, cache_object=None, **kwargs):

1325:         cache_object if available.

1330:                 category=RemovedInWagtail90Warning,

1356:         ``(site_id, None, None)`` if ``NoReverseMatch`` exception is raised.

1358:         This is used internally by the ``full_url``, ``url``, ``relative_url``
1359:         and ``get_site`` properties and methods; pages with custom URL routing
1360:         should override this method in order to have those operations return
1361:         the custom URLs.

1363:         Accepts an optional keyword argument ``request``, which may be used
1364:         to avoid repeated database / cache lookups. Typically, a page model
1365:         that overrides ``get_url_parts`` should not need to deal with
1366:         ``request`` directly, and should just pass it to the original method
1367:         when calling ``super``.

1371: 
1372:         if not possible_sites:

1374: 
1375:         # Thanks to the ordering applied by Site.get_site_root_paths(),
1376:         # the first item is ideal in the vast majority of setups.
1377:         site_id, root_path, root_url, language_code = possible_sites[0]

1379:         unique_site_ids = {values[0] for values in possible_sites}

1392:

1397:             try:

1399:                     get_supported_content_language_variant(translation.get_language())
1400:                     == language_code
1401:                 ):
1402:                     language_code = translation.get_language()
1403:             except LookupError:
1404:                 # active language code is not a recognised content language, so leave
1405:                 # page's language code unchanged
1406:                 pass
1407: 
1408:         # The page may not be routable because wagtail_serve is not registered
1409:         # This may be the case if Wagtail is used headless
1410:         try:
1411:             if use_wagtail_i18n:
1412:                 with translation.override(language_code):
1413:                     page_path = reverse(
1414:                         "wagtail_serve", args=(self.url_path[len(root_path) :],)
1415:                     )
1416:             else:
1417:                 page_path = reverse(
1418:                     "wagtail_serve", args=(self.url_path[len(root_path) :],)
1419:                 )
1420:         except NoReverseMatch:
1421:             return (site_id, None, None)
1422: 
1423:         # Remove the trailing slash from the URL reverse generates if
1424:         # WAGTAIL_APPEND_SLASH is False and we're not trying to serve
1425:         # the root path
1426:         if not WAGTAIL_APPEND_SLASH and page_path != "/":
1427:             page_path = page_path.rstrip("/")
1428: 
1429:         return (site_id, root_url, page_path)

1434:         """
1435:         url_parts = self.get_url_parts(request=request)
1436: 
1437:         if url_parts is None or url_parts[1] is None and url_parts[2] is None:

1447:     def get_url(self, request=None, current_site=None):
1448:         """
1449:         Return the 'most appropriate' URL for referring to this page from the pages we serve,
1450:         within the Wagtail backend and actual website templates;
1451:         this is the local URL (starting with '/') if we're only running a single site
1452:         (i.e. we know that whatever the current page is being served from, this link will be on the
1453:         same domain), and the full URL (with domain) if not.
1454:         Return ``None`` if the page is not routable.
1455: 
1456:         Accepts an optional but recommended ``request`` keyword argument that, if provided, will
1457:         be used to cache site-level URL information (thereby avoiding repeated database / cache
1458:         lookups) and, via the ``Site.find_for_request()`` function, determine whether a relative
1459:         or full URL is most appropriate.
1460:         """
1461:         # ``current_site`` is purposefully undocumented, as one can simply pass the request and get
1462:         # a relative URL based on ``Site.find_for_request()``. Nonetheless, support it here to avoid
1463:         # copy/pasting the code to the ``relative_url`` method below.
1464:         if current_site is None and request is not None:
1465:             site = Site.find_for_request(request)
1466:             current_site = site
1467:         url_parts = self.get_url_parts(request=request)
1468: 
1469:         if url_parts is None or url_parts[1] is None and url_parts[2] is None:
1470:             # page is not routable
1471:             return
1472: 
1473:         site_id, root_url, page_path = url_parts
1474: 
1475:         # Get number of unique sites in root paths
1476:         # Note: there may be more root paths to sites if there are multiple languages
1477:         num_sites = len(
1478:             {root_path[0] for root_path in self._get_site_root_paths(request)}
1479:         )
1480: 
1481:         if (current_site is not None and site_id == current_site.id) or num_sites == 1:
1482:             # the site matches OR we're only running a single site, so a local URL is sufficient
1483:             return page_path
1484:         else:
1485:             return root_url + page_path
1486:

1490:         """
1491:         Return the 'most appropriate' URL for this page taking into account the site we're currently on;
1492:         a local URL if the site matches, or a fully qualified one otherwise.
1493:         Return ``None`` if the page is not routable.
1494: 
1495:         Accepts an optional but recommended ``request`` keyword argument that, if provided, will
1496:         be used to cache site-level URL information (thereby avoiding repeated database / cache
1497:         lookups).
1498:         """
1499:         return self.get_url(request=request, current_site=current_site)

1620:     @classmethod
1621:     def can_exist_under(cls, parent):
1622:         """
1623:         Checks if this page type can exist as a subpage under a parent page
1624:         instance.
1625: 
1626:         See also: :func:`Page.can_create_at` and :func:`Page.can_move_to`
1627:         """
1628:         return cls in parent.specific_class.allowed_subpage_models()

1630:     @classmethod
1631:     def can_create_at(cls, parent):
1632:         """
1633:         Checks if this page type can be created as a subpage under a parent
1634:         page instance.
1635:         """
1636:         can_create = cls.is_creatable and cls.can_exist_under(parent)
1637: 
1638:         if cls.max_count is not None:
1639:             can_create = can_create and cls.objects.count() < cls.max_count
1640: 
1641:         if cls.max_count_per_parent is not None:
1642:             can_create = (
1643:                 can_create
1644:                 and parent.get_children().type(cls).count() < cls.max_count_per_parent
1645:             )
1646: 
1647:         return can_create
1648: 
1649:     def can_move_to(self, parent):
1650:         """
1651:         Checks if this page instance can be moved to be a subpage of a parent
1652:         page instance.
1653:         """
1654:         # Prevent pages from being moved to different language sections
1655:         # The only page that can have multi-lingual children is the root page
1656:         parent_is_root = parent.depth == 1
1657:         if not parent_is_root and parent.locale_id != self.locale_id:
1658:             return False
1659: 
1660:         # Must be able to exist under parent
1661:         if not self.can_exist_under(parent):
1662:             return False
1663: 
1664:         # If the page has a max_count_per_parent, check for a page of this type under the destination
1665:         if self.max_count_per_parent is not None:
1666:             return (
1667:                 parent.get_children().type(self.specific_class).not_page(self).count()
1668:                 < self.max_count_per_parent
1669:             )
1670: 
1671:         return True

1925:     def get_view_restrictions(self):
1926:         """
1927:         Return a query set of all page view restrictions that apply to this page.
1928: 
1929:         This checks the current page and all ancestor pages for page view restrictions.
1930: 
1931:         If any of those pages are aliases, it will resolve them to their source pages
1932:         before querying PageViewRestrictions so alias pages use the same view restrictions
1933:         as their source page and they cannot have their own.
1934:         """
1935:         page_ids_to_check = set()
1936: 
1937:         def add_page_to_check_list(page):
1938:             # If the page is an alias, add the source page to the check list instead
1939:             if page.alias_of:
1940:                 add_page_to_check_list(page.alias_of)
1941:             else:
1942:                 page_ids_to_check.add(page.id)
1943: 
1944:         # Check current page for view restrictions
1945:         add_page_to_check_list(self)
1946: 
1947:         # Check each ancestor for view restrictions as well
1948:         for page in self.get_ancestors().only("alias_of"):
1949:             add_page_to_check_list(page)
1950: 
1951:         return PageViewRestriction.objects.filter(page_id__in=page_ids_to_check)

1955:     def serve_password_required_response(self, request, form, action_url):
1956:         """
1957:         Serve a response indicating that the user has been denied access to view this page,
1958:         and must supply a password.
1959:         ``form`` = a Django form object containing the password input
1960:             (and zero or more hidden fields that also need to be output on the template)
1961:         ``action_url`` = URL that this form should be POSTed to
1962:         """
1963: 
1964:         password_required_template = self.password_required_template or getattr(
1965:             settings,
1966:             "WAGTAIL_PASSWORD_REQUIRED_TEMPLATE",
1967:             "wagtailcore/password_required.html",
1968:         )
1969: 
1970:         # Ensuring preview attributes exist
1971:         if not hasattr(request, "is_preview"):
1972:             request.is_preview = False
1973:         if not hasattr(request, "preview_mode"):
1974:             request.preview_mode = None
1975: 
1976:         context = self.get_context(request)
1977:         context["form"] = form
1978:         context["action_url"] = action_url
1979:         return TemplateResponse(request, password_required_template, context)

2188: class PagePermissionTester:
2189:     def __init__(self, user, page):
2190:         from wagtail.permissions import page_permission_policy
2191: 
2192:         self.user = user
2193:         self.permission_policy = page_permission_policy
2194:         self.page = page
2195:         self.page_is_root = page.depth == 1  # Equivalent to page.is_root()
2196: 
2197:         if self.user.is_active and not self.user.is_superuser:
2198:             self.permissions = {
2199:                 # Get the 'action' part of the permission codename, e.g.
2200:                 # 'add' instead of 'add_page'
2201:                 perm.permission.codename.rsplit("_", maxsplit=1)[0]
2202:                 for perm in self.permission_policy.get_cached_permissions_for_user(user)
2203:                 if self.page.path.startswith(perm.page.path)
2204:             }
2205: 
2206:     def user_has_lock(self):
2207:         return self.page.locked_by_id == self.user.pk
2208: 
2209:     def page_locked(self):
2210:         lock = self.page.get_lock()
2211:         return lock and lock.for_user(self.user)
2212: 
2213:     def can_add_subpage(self):
2214:         if not self.user.is_active:
2215:             return False
2216: 
2217:         specific_class = self.page.specific_class
2218:         if specific_class is None:
2219:             return False
2220: 
2221:         creatable_subpage_models = specific_class.creatable_subpage_models()
2222:         if not any(
2223:             subpage_model.can_create_at(self.page)
2224:             for subpage_model in creatable_subpage_models
2225:         ):
2226:             return False
2227:         return self.user.is_superuser or ("add" in self.permissions)
2228: 
2229:     def can_edit(self):
2230:         if not self.user.is_active:
2231:             return False
2232: 
2233:         if (
2234:             self.page_is_root
2235:         ):  # root node is not a page and can never be edited, even by superusers
2236:             return False
2237: 
2238:         if self.user.is_superuser:
2239:             return True
2240: 
2241:         if "change" in self.permissions:
2242:             return True
2243: 
2244:         if "add" in self.permissions and self.page.owner_id == self.user.pk:
2245:             return True
2246: 
2247:         current_workflow_task = self.page.current_workflow_task
2248:         if current_workflow_task:
2249:             if current_workflow_task.user_can_access_editor(self.page, self.user):
2250:                 return True
2251: 
2252:         return False
2253: 
2254:     def can_delete(self, ignore_bulk=False):
2255:         if not self.user.is_active:
2256:             return False
2257: 
2258:         if (
2259:             self.page_is_root
2260:         ):  # root node is not a page and can never be deleted, even by superusers
2261:             return False
2262: 
2263:         if self.user.is_superuser:
2264:             # superusers require no further checks
2265:             return True
2266: 
2267:         # if the user does not have bulk_delete permission, they may only delete leaf pages
2268:         if (
2269:             "bulk_delete" not in self.permissions
2270:             and not self.page.is_leaf()
2271:             and not ignore_bulk
2272:         ):
2273:             return False
2274: 
2275:         if "change" in self.permissions:
2276:             # if the user does not have publish permission, we also need to confirm that there
2277:             # are no published pages here
2278:             if "publish" not in self.permissions:
2279:                 pages_to_delete = self.page.get_descendants(inclusive=True)
2280:                 if pages_to_delete.live().exists():
2281:                     return False
2282: 
2283:             return True
2284: 
2285:         elif "add" in self.permissions:
2286:             pages_to_delete = self.page.get_descendants(inclusive=True)
2287:             if "publish" in self.permissions:
2288:                 # we don't care about live state, but all pages must be owned by this user
2289:                 # (i.e. eliminating pages owned by this user must give us the empty set)
2290:                 return not pages_to_delete.exclude(owner=self.user).exists()
2291:             else:
2292:                 # all pages must be owned by this user and non-live
2293:                 # (i.e. eliminating non-live pages owned by this user must give us the empty set)
2294:                 return not pages_to_delete.exclude(live=False, owner=self.user).exists()
2295: 
2296:         else:
2297:             return False

2358:     def can_publish_subpage(self):
2359:         """
2360:         Niggly special case for creating and publishing a page in one go.
2361:         Differs from can_publish in that we want to be able to publish subpages of root, but not
2362:         to be able to publish root itself. (Also, can_publish_subpage returns false if the page
2363:         does not allow subpages at all.)
2364:         """
2365:         if not self.user.is_active:
2366:             return False
2367:         specific_class = self.page.specific_class
2368:         if specific_class is None or not specific_class.creatable_subpage_models():
2369:             return False

2371:         return self.user.is_superuser or ("publish" in self.permissions)

2373:     def can_reorder_children(self):
2374:         """
2375:         Reorder permission checking is similar to publishing a subpage, since it immediately
2376:         affects published pages. However, it shouldn't care about the 'creatability' of
2377:         page types, because the action only ever updates existing pages.
2378:         """
2379:         if not self.user.is_active:
2380:             return False
2381:         return self.user.is_superuser or ("publish" in self.permissions)

2383:     def can_move(self):
2384:         """
2385:         Moving a page should be logically equivalent to deleting and re-adding it (and all its children).
2386:         As such, the permission test for 'can this be moved at all?' should be the same as for deletion.
2387:         (Further constraints will then apply on where it can be moved *to*.)
2388:         """
2389:         return self.can_delete(ignore_bulk=True)
2390: 
2391:     def can_copy(self):
2392:         return self.can_edit()
2393: 
2394:     def can_move_to(self, destination):
2395:         # reject the logically impossible cases first
2396:         if self.page == destination or destination.is_descendant_of(self.page):
2397:             return False
2398: 
2399:         # reject moves that are forbidden by subpage_types / parent_page_types rules
2400:         # (these rules apply to superusers too)
2401:         # – but only check this if the page is not already under the target parent.
2402:         # If it already is, then the user is just reordering the page, and we want
2403:         # to allow it even if the page currently violates the subpage_type /
2404:         # parent_page_type rules. This can happen if it was either created before
2405:         # the rules were specified, or it was done programmatically (e.g. to
2406:         # predefine a set of pages and disallow the creation of new subpages by
2407:         # setting subpage_types = []).
2408: 
2409:         if (not self.page.is_child_of(destination)) and (
2410:             not self.page.specific.can_move_to(destination)
2411:         ):
2412:             return False
2413: 
2414:         # shortcut the trivial 'everything' / 'nothing' permissions
2415:         if not self.user.is_active:
2416:             return False
2417:         if self.user.is_superuser:
2418:             return True
2419: 
2420:         # check that the page can be moved at all
2421:         if not self.can_move():
2422:             return False
2423: 
2424:         # Inspect permissions on the destination
2425:         destination_perms = destination.permissions_for_user(self.user)
2426: 
2427:         # we always need at least add permission in the target
2428:         if "add" not in destination_perms.permissions:
2429:             return False
2430: 
2431:         if self.page.live or self.page.get_descendants().filter(live=True).exists():
2432:             # moving this page will entail publishing within the destination section
2433:             return "publish" in destination_perms.permissions
2434:         else:
2435:             # no publishing required, so the already-tested 'add' permission is sufficient
2436:             return True
2437: 
2438:     def can_copy_to(self, destination, recursive=False):
2439:         if not self.can_copy():
2440:             return False
2441: 
2442:         # reject the logically impossible cases first
2443:         # recursive can't copy to the same tree otherwise it will be on infinite loop
2444:         if recursive and (
2445:             self.page == destination or destination.is_descendant_of(self.page)
2446:         ):
2447:             return False
2448: 
2449:         # reject inactive users early
2450:         if not self.user.is_active:
2451:             return False
2452: 
2453:         # reject early if pages of this type cannot be created at the destination
2454:         if not self.page.specific_class.can_create_at(destination):
2455:             return False
2456: 
2457:         # skip permission checking for super users
2458:         if self.user.is_superuser:
2459:             return True
2460: 
2461:         # Inspect permissions on the destination
2462:         destination_perms = destination.permissions_for_user(self.user)
2463: 
2464:         return destination_perms.can_add_subpage()

2466:     def can_view_revisions(self):
2467:         return not self.page_is_root

2604:     def viewable_by_user(self, user):
